surface.title: Why Chinese Is a Denser Language Than English — And What That Means for AI
surface.text: Every character of Chinese carries roughly four times the information of an English character. This isn't a cultural observation — it's an information-theoretic fact with direct implications for how large language models tokenize, process, and generate text across languages. In this article, I break down the entropy measurements, explain why BPE tokenizers partially compensate for the gap, and argue that current multilingual models are systematically undertrained on high-entropy languages.

content.description: Per-character Shannon entropy comparison across languages and implications for LLM tokenization efficiency and training data allocation

code.source:
from collections import Counter
import math

def char_entropy(text):
    """Compute per-character Shannon entropy in bits."""
    counts = Counter(text)
    total = sum(counts.values())
    return -sum(
        (c / total) * math.log2(c / total)
        for c in counts.values()
    )

# Extended analysis: entropy per token accounting for BPE tokenization
def effective_token_entropy(char_entropy, avg_chars_per_token):
    """Information content per BPE token."""
    return char_entropy * avg_chars_per_token

# English: ~1.0 bits/char, ~4.0 chars/token → 4.0 bits/token
# Chinese: ~4.2 bits/char, ~1.5 chars/token → 6.3 bits/token
# Chinese tokens carry 58% more information despite being shorter

print(f"English: {effective_token_entropy(1.0, 4.0):.1f} bits/token")
print(f"Chinese: {effective_token_entropy(4.2, 1.5):.1f} bits/token")
print(f"Ratio: {effective_token_entropy(4.2, 1.5) / effective_token_entropy(1.0, 4.0):.2f}x")

data.source: Shannon (1951) original estimate for English; Wu & Liu (2023) for modern Mandarin corpus analysis; GPT-4 tokenizer statistics from OpenAI (2024)
data.methodology: Character-level frequency analysis on 10M+ character corpora per language. Token lengths measured on 1M-token samples per language using cl100k_base tokenizer.
data.range: English: Reuters corpus + Wikipedia (2020-2025). Chinese: Xinhua + Baidu Baike (2020-2025).
data.notes: BPE tokenization partially compensates for character-level entropy differences, but a 58% gap remains at the token level. This suggests multilingual models need proportionally more Chinese training data per token to achieve equivalent performance.
